figures.caption: Fig. 2. A visualization of the acceptance region in rejection sampling for an additive white Gaussian noise channel Y = X+N with X ∼ N (0, 0.75) and N ∼ N (0, 0.25), where X and Y are truncated to a large but finite range to ensure a bounded density ratio. Here, x = −0.5 and the two proposal samples are Y1 = 0.3, Y2 = −0.4 with heights L1 = 1.5 and L2 = 1.1, where we use the shorthand Li ≜ MUi. We see that x ̸∈ S1 but x ∈ S2, meaning Y1 is rejected and Y2 is accepted. The PX -probability of hitting the set Si is exactly wYi (Li).
